PostToolUse.name: docker-compose-changed
PostToolUse.description: When docker-compose.yml is modified, suggest recreating containers

hooks.message:
docker-compose file was modified. Recreate containers to apply changes:

```bash
# Recreate with updated config
docker-compose -f docker-compose.{project}.yml up -d

# If service definitions changed
docker-compose -f docker-compose.{project}.yml down && docker-compose -f docker-compose.{project}.yml up -d

# If Dockerfile changed
docker-compose -f docker-compose.{project}.yml up -d --build
```

PostToolUse.name: dockerfile-changed
PostToolUse.description: When Dockerfile is modified, suggest rebuilding the image

hooks.message:
Dockerfile was modified. Rebuild the image to apply changes:

```bash
# Rebuild and restart
docker-compose build && docker-compose up -d

# Clean rebuild (no cache)
docker-compose build --no-cache && docker-compose up -d

# Or use plugin command
/docker-build --version {VERSION}
```

PostToolUse.name: nginx-conf-changed
PostToolUse.description: When nginx.conf is modified, suggest restarting nginx

hooks.message:
nginx.conf was modified. Reload nginx to apply:

```bash
# Reload nginx config (no downtime)
docker-compose exec nginx nginx -s reload

# Or restart nginx container
docker-compose restart nginx

# Test config first
docker-compose exec nginx nginx -t
```

Also ensure proxy_mode = True in odoo.conf when using nginx.

PostToolUse.name: env-file-changed
PostToolUse.description: When .env is modified, suggest container restart

hooks.message:
.env file was modified. Recreate containers to apply new environment variables:

```bash
docker-compose down && docker-compose up -d
```

Note: `docker-compose restart` does NOT pick up .env changes. You must `down` then `up`.

PostToolUse.name: entrypoint-changed
PostToolUse.description: When entrypoint.sh is modified, remind about chmod +x and rebuild

hooks.message:
entrypoint.sh was modified. Ensure it's executable and rebuild:

```bash
# Make executable (important!)
chmod +x entrypoint.sh

# Rebuild image (entrypoint is COPY'd into image)
docker-compose build && docker-compose up -d
```

If the entrypoint is volume-mounted (not COPY'd), a container restart is sufficient.

PostToolUse.name: odoo-conf-docker
PostToolUse.description: When an Odoo .conf file in Docker context is modified

hooks.message:
Odoo configuration file was modified. Restart the Odoo container:

```bash
docker-compose restart odoo
```

Key settings to verify:
- data_dir = /var/lib/odoo (CRITICAL)
- workers >= 2 (production)
- proxy_mode = True (behind nginx)
- gevent_port = 8072 (v17+) or longpolling_port = 8072 (v14-16)

OnError.name: error-port-allocated
OnError.description: Port already allocated  -  suggest stopping conflicting containers
OnError.matcher: port is already allocated|Bind for.*failed.*port is already allocated|address already in use|Error starting userland proxy

hooks.message:
Port conflict detected. Another container or process is using the same port.

Fix:
```bash
# Find what's using the port
docker ps --format 'table {{.Names}}\t{{.Ports}}' | grep 8069

# Stop conflicting containers
docker-compose down

# Or change ports in .env
# HTTP_PORT=8169

# Windows: kill process on port
netstat -ano | findstr :8069
taskkill /PID {PID} /F

# Linux: kill process on port
lsof -ti:8069 | xargs kill -9
```

OnError.name: error-no-space
OnError.description: No space left on device  -  suggest Docker cleanup
OnError.matcher: no space left on device|ENOSPC|disk space|No space left

hooks.message:
Docker disk space exhausted.

Fix:
```bash
# Check what's using space
docker system df

# Remove unused containers, networks, images
docker system prune -f

# More aggressive (removes ALL unused images)
docker system prune -af

# Remove unused volumes (WARNING: data loss)
docker volume prune -f

# Check specific large images
docker images --format 'table {{.Repository}}:{{.Tag}}\t{{.Size}}' | sort -k2 -h
```

OnError.name: error-502-bad-gateway
OnError.description: 502 Bad Gateway  -  check Odoo container health and nginx upstream

hooks.message:
502 Bad Gateway  -  nginx cannot reach Odoo.

Debug:
```bash
# 1. Check if Odoo container is running
docker ps | grep web

# 2. Check Odoo health
docker exec {container} curl -sf http://localhost:8069/web/health

# 3. Check Odoo logs for errors
docker logs {odoo_container} --tail 50

# 4. Check nginx upstream config
docker exec {nginx_container} cat /etc/nginx/conf.d/default.conf | grep upstream

# 5. Ensure containers are on same network
docker network inspect {network}
```

Common causes:
- Odoo still starting (check start_period in healthcheck)
- Odoo crashed (check logs for Python errors)
- Wrong upstream name in nginx.conf

OnError.name: error-oom-killed
OnError.description: Container killed by OOM  -  suggest increasing resource limits

hooks.message:
Container killed by Out of Memory (OOM).

Fix in docker-compose:
```yaml
services:
  odoo:
    deploy:
      resources:
        limits:
          memory: 2G    # Increase this
          cpus: '2.0'
        reservations:
          memory: 512M
```

Also check odoo.conf:
```ini
limit_memory_soft = 2147483648   ; 2 GB
limit_memory_hard = 2684354560   ; 2.5 GB
workers = 2                       ; Reduce if memory constrained
```

Run /docker-perf for detailed analysis.

OnError.name: error-source-not-found
OnError.description: Odoo source not found in container
OnError.matcher: Odoo source not found|No such file.*odoo-bin|No such file.*setup/odoo|ModuleNotFoundError.*odoo

hooks.message:
Odoo source code is not mounted correctly.

Check:
```bash
# 1. Verify source exists on host
ls -la sources/odoo-19/setup/odoo  # v19
ls -la sources/odoo-17/odoo-bin    # v14-18

# 2. Check docker-compose volume mount
# Should be: ./sources/odoo-19:/opt/odoo/source:ro

# 3. Verify inside container
docker exec {container} ls -la /opt/odoo/source/
```

Common fixes:
- Clone Odoo source: git clone -b 19.0 git@github.com:taqat-techno/odoo.git sources/odoo-19
- Fix volume path in docker-compose.yml
